message:
fix(rpc): improve requestId/id alias fallback in extension_ui_response parsing and expand unit tests

The previous implementation chained requestId and id through a single
`or_else` expression, which meant when requestId was present but not a
valid string (e.g., an integer), the trim/filter applied to requestId's
None would short-circuit before the id alias could be attempted. This
refactored version separates the two lookups so the id alias fallback
is properly attempted when requestId resolves to None for any reason
(missing, non-string type, empty, or whitespace-only).

src/rpc.rs — rpc_parse_extension_ui_response_id:
- Extracted requestId lookup into a standalone binding with trim + empty filter
- Added or_else closure for the id alias path with identical trim + empty filter
- This ensures id alias is attempted when requestId is null, non-string, empty,
  or whitespace-only — matching upstream TypeScript behavior

src/rpc.rs — new unit tests:
- parse_ui_response_id_prefers_request_id_over_id_alias: when both present,
  requestId wins
- parse_ui_response_id_falls_back_to_id_alias_when_request_id_not_string:
  non-string requestId (integer) triggers id fallback
- parse_ui_response_id_falls_back_to_id_alias_when_request_id_blank:
  empty string requestId triggers id fallback
- parse_ui_response_id_falls_back_to_id_alias_when_request_id_whitespace:
  whitespace-only requestId triggers id fallback

src/rpc.rs — try_send_line_with_backpressure tests:
- try_send_line_with_backpressure_waits_until_capacity_is_available: verifies
  the function blocks and retries when the channel is full, then delivers once
  the receiver drains
- try_send_line_with_backpressure_preserves_large_payload: verifies a 256KB
  payload is transmitted intact through the backpressure path

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
